reviews.review_description:
I like puzzle games, I’ve played a few of the “open the box” type. This one is really well done - the puzzles are genuinely challenging, especially the ones that unlock after you complete the story mode. The story itself is actually quite interesting and makes for a nice bonus.

The controls leave a bit to be desired. I’ve played in both modes: controllers and hand tracking - but unfortunately, neither is perfect. It seems to me that devs unnecessarily introduced hand tracking instead of focusing on the controllers. Despite that, I’m giving it a 5 because it’s one of the most challenging “open the box” games I’ve played.
The puzzles are nice. The storyline is fine. The environments are ok. Overall the game is good. My issue is that the rotation puzzle mechanic are a bit hard to grasp.

reviews.review_title: I’ve got my mind set about this game
reviews.review_description: Starts out like a decent puzzler then rapidly become repetitive. Voiceover work is bland and one dimensional. Puzzles then start to quickly get bugged. Pull out a tab and others go in — except you let go and they just spaz out and you’ve almost gotta grab them to reset it. Not a puzzle element — just buggy. Not fun. Seems like there was no thought out story. Reached a puzzle that was game breaking and gave up. I’m not the only one reading the reviews. Wish I could refund. Avoid!

UPDATE: I've found that the controllers work much better. That said, I've now gotten to a puzzle that's literally impossible..
A new mechanic was introduced that has the needed poles (or whatever you call the arms you stick the gears on) ONLY pop out if you have the cube turned in very specific ways. This would be fine, but the directions it needs to be facing for the 2 different sides of the cube I need to use (moving/ starting gear to to the side housing the stationary gear you work towards) are POLAR OPPOSITE from eachother! Get one side correct, and the others become inaccessible and vice-versa, even if you have gears on them already (it throws the off).
There's NO WAY around this! Even for $10, I can't keep a title that has such an early dead-end. I'd advise anyone else to save their money as well...


OG: The game has a nice premise and the puzzles in-themselves are nice. My issue is with the hand tracking making a simple puzzle much harder than it needed to be. I have to fight half the time to grab the cube in the correct spot to even turn it, and grabbing and letting go of the gears is generally awkward. I've not tried the controllers to play yet, as I've heard that setup is somehow worse (and from a development team that KNKWS how to implement basic controller mechanics...), but hopefully I'll somehow have a better experience with them.
I think it's cool this has a narrative of some kind, but I have noticed the dialogue is slightly off, and the voice acting mostly terrible (mainly fromthe female doctor who's name escapes me atm. The player character isn't great per se, but is fine and gets the job done. The woman sounds about as "human" as talking to Siri or Alexa do on your devices. Like you're literally listening to a.i. read these lines...).
So those distract from the narrative, but hopefully I'll just get used to it the morecI hear them/ her talk.
That is, if I decide to keep the game at all. The hand tracking features just make me feel as if I'm fighting the game a bit more than solving the puzzles, making something that would otherwise be relaxing and rewarding feel incredibly frustrating half the time...

I'm grateful it's fairly priced, but even a few dollars is too much for something your not able to really enjoy. So I'm gonna try it again and hope for the best, as I would love to be able to enjoy this title.

developer_response.body:
Hi Teele891,

Thank you for playing our game and leaving a thorough review! Our team will try to make some adjustments to the pins and improve the gear grabbing - we hope you will try out the game once an update drops. Thank you once again for taking the time to write the review!

reviews.review_title: Great game with some strong issues
reviews.review_description: I love the game and the approche. Very great handtracking and the story keeps me hanging on. But what is a clear No Go here is that the progress is not saved. It might be, I dont know, but I had to restart the game two times now, first because I wanted to see what the challenges are second time because I just found some random buttons that did not make any sence and pressed them wich lead me to the main screen without saving. Best would be to make a clear notification that all saved prgress will be lost and let players confirm again so that problem will be solved.

developer_response.body:
Hi Simeonski,

Thank you for leaving a review and we're glad you are enjoying the game! The save issue is something we haven't encountered before, but we will investigate. Thank you for mentioning this; you can also reach us on our Discord where you can discuss with us directly: https://discord.gg/becomeawizard 

Cheers!

reviews.review_description:
It's an interesting to do puzzles and I enjoyed the experience.  It took a while to figure out the controls (I used the controllers instead of hand-tracking) and it's annoying that I can't grab gears with my left hand.

More frustrating was the story.  It started really interesting only to have attitudes of NPCs change for no apparent reason and then end abruptly with no conclusion.  The story lays out clues ("oh, those numbers mean something") then never follows up.

developer_response.body:
Hi Thebcwhite,

Thank you for leaving a review! We're glad you enjoyed the puzzles and the overall experience :)

reviews.review_description:
I can't get the button things to come out. 
If I could figure that out. I'd love it

developer_response.body:
Hi Serilda,

Thank you for leaving a review! We're sorry you are having trouble playing the game, could you visit our Discord (https://discord.com/invite/becomeawizard) and reach out to us? We will do what we can to help you enjoy the game again! Cheers!

reviews.review_description: Just purchased this game and enjoyed the 1st puzzle box but the 2nd box after fixing a few cogs the bottom half of the box disappeared so now I can't see where to put the cogs. Very frustrating glitch

developer_response.body: Thank you for bringing this issue to our attention! We will try to investigate this as soon as possible and make any necessary corrections.

reviews.review_description:
This game is fun so far. I love a good puzzle game and this one is a great score for the price! I am super impressed by how well the handtracking works. There is a story line as you work thru adding cogs to cubes, each one growing increasingly more difficult. 

I still have plenty left to play but for just starting, I'd say this is one to add to your puzzle game collection.

developer_response.body: Hi, GoddessRobin! Hand Tracking technology indeed plays a huge role in Mindset, so we're glad you're having fun using it! We wish you the best luck with next dices!

reviews.review_description:
I tried this game out, excited to see the hand tracking and such as I played. First off, for a game that only needs to have grabbing the hand tracking seems busted, like I should only need three fingers to hold a gear but instead I have to close my entire hand which feels off with the scale of the gears. Part of the time I grab a gear but instead start tilting the box itself
 Secondly, the story feels out of place for what the game is, story driven puzzle games are a thing but this just feels like random story elements not related to the game itself. Do not reccomend, better puzzle games out there, let alone with hand tracking support.

Mindset is a puzzle box game involving gears at first glance. Then the narative story draws you deep into the mystery of unlocking your mind as well.

This game can be enjoyed as strickly a puzzle game, or a puzzle and mysterious story that unfolds.

The puzzles are fun and challendging and will have you really thinking. The game can be played with hand tracking or motion controls. If using hand tracking, be in a well lit area.

I find the best gameplay is in the story mode. It gives me Outer Limits / Lost vibes. You just know there is something big going on.

Check this game out for a fantastic good time and mystery.

Watch my preview here,
https://youtu.be/tTH6ONpetCY

developer_response.body:
Hey, IronbugVR! 

Thank you for your awesome review and factual, engaging video! As a game developer, we simply love this kind of feedback, so we really appreciate your work 👍😉 Cheers!

reviews.review_description: So this game doesn't have the finest graphics (which, neither does the most popular mini golf game.... this has a similar feel) and the storyline has some interesting dialogue (much like other more complex games -  RM1 and RM2 for abbreviation - both we've played with great reviews).... so I cannot suggest that this game doesn't belong in the big leagues.  For the price, it is an absolute steal.  I found it addictive and rewarding, as you essentially complete your puzzlers in stages, advancing to new levels/locations.  Oh!  And we discovered that you can solve each puzzle in different ways, sometimes even ending up with extra pieces!   We couldn't put it down!  I like to play my single-player puzzlers as multiplayer in case I get stuck, so I launched with a partner and we talked through any challenges we faced (waiting for one another if we fell behind).  It is a great game and certainly replayable.  We haven't finished it, which was hard to do because we didn't want to stop.  Highly recommended!  Do we see places and refinements in the graphics and text quality where it can be improved?  Sure.  But not for one second did that effect our gameplay.  There were a couple moments when the menu popped up asking if we wanted to "resume" (which we panicked, out of fear of losing our progress)... but it was easy enough to resume, and the progress auto-saves to reduce your fears of any losses anyway.  You won't regret this one.

developer_response.body: Hey, Muggito! Thank you for such an extensive and specific review! We're glad to hear that you enjoyed the main game mechanics and that you had a good time with Mindset. Solving cube-shaped puzzles can be really addictive, but no worries, as there are dozens of them waiting to be opened!

reviews.review_title: Hand Tracking isn’t there yet, but…
reviews.review_description:
Flawed but highly entertaining and suitably priced. 
Full video review here https://youtu.be/065ALbVfuWc

developer_response.body: Hey, TheLabVideoGameTV! Thank you for your awesome video review! And hey - we're very happy that you consider Mindset a must-play title for all VR puzzle games fans and hand tracking technology enthusiasts 🙌

reviews.review_title: Cool mix of story telling with puzzles
reviews.review_description: Another hand tracking game! This game is a story telling with a hint of puzzles. I also love the voice actors/actress. The only thing It bums me is while there's a controller support, you cant move the cube like when you can using hand tracking. The puzzles isnt hard enough to make you stuck too long. This could be also a fun game for kids.

Lovely new hidear. For vr .I just whish that has it gets really hard  the people which make minset could give us clues. Not everyone  are brilliant gamers . but thay  love games  .and willing to learn

This is seriously an amazing puzzle game. The puzzles can be tricky but so satisfying when completed. 
The graphics were really nice and I felt like they were not distracting while doing the puzzle. I couldn't help myself every time I entered a new environment that i needed to check out everything especially when your surroundings are part of the story. 
The story is so good that it keeps you intrigued. I have not finished the game but I can't wait to  find out.
The hand tracking is amazing I can honestly say. When I usually have a hand tracking game it can be a bit frustrating but in mindset I didn't feel that way at all. When I first started I knew immediately it was me doing something wrong when picking up pieces but once I was into my 3rd puzzle it was so good. 
I am really enjoying Mindset and can't wait to get back in there !!!

Great story great challenges but some challenges r the buggies that u hav to figur out how to get around them. Basically handing tracking and controllers hav to b used or for me. But for sur worth the price good job 2 developers haven't done the challenge mode yet but definitely cking to c if maybe the bugs will get up outta this one lol. Every games buggy here and there and even buggy very fair price but fingers crossed. Handtracking dnt wk out to great for me but switching bk and forth is a pain, opps forgot the connectors when fears r put on flip upside down so u can not grab them at all but takes geR bk off and here we go again lmk but good job on it! Fun and very challenging for me anyway poster child for adhd so... lol
Enjoyed trying to get all the cogs in the right place.  A fun challenging game ♡

developer_response.body:
Hey, SomeArtsyGirl! 

Thank you for your review! 🤩 We're glad you're having fun solving the cube-shaped puzzles... be careful, though, as the more you solve, the more challenging it gets!

reviews.review_description:
The game is actually a fun concept of puzzles and the story is intriguing, It can jump ahead of itself sometimes but I don't see that as a problem.

My issues is that while controllers are supported, they are clearly not intergrated for the game. Which becomes very clear at the early stages of the game. 

However, my biggest complaint was the positioning of my character. I find I am constantly needing to adjust my position or height all the time to simply work with the puzzle box in front of me. I also find that my default position merges me into the title text OR the box itself, requiring me to dance around once again. Meaning that sitting down is an uncomfortable experience.

My recommendation is allowing the ability to move the box via controllers and to have the box be able to move by grabbing it with both hands for hand tracking.

There also is a lack of a settings selection in the main menu and attempting to reach the main menu while mid puzzle box leaves me with a black screen (Restarting the app resolved it)

Overall, This game definitely had some interesting puzzles, but the amount of fidgiting and handling made my overall experience uncomfortable and unrewarding, even when I solved a couple of boxes. I hope that in future patches, this game will become the roaring success that it deserves to be!

developer_response.body: Thank you for your comprehensive, factual review! We are glad that you like the concept of our game and that you find it interesting (and things get even cooler as the storyline unfolds!) As for the positioning issues - thank you for bringing them to our attention. We will be sure to test them again. Please remember that with your right thumb (OK sign) you can bring up the positioning menu - perhaps this will prove helpful. Cheers!
